title: JARVIS Step 32 — Live End-to-End Read-Only Conversation Smoke Test
purpose: Verify the committed Step 31 dashboard UI and Step 30 POST /api/jarvis/ask backend work together safely. Validation/report-only: no app.py, template, classifier, endpoint, or feature changes.

head.subject: Add JARVIS Step 31 read-only ask UI wiring

execution_environment_note: A long-running app instance is listening on the normal JARVIS port 8765, but it is STALE: it was started before Step 30, so over real HTTP it returns 404 for GET and POST /api/jarvis/ask (the route did not exist when that process started). The committed app.py DOES register the route (app.py:8445). To smoke the COMMITTED code faithfully without disrupting the user's running process, the end-to-end probes were run in-process against the same FastAPI ASGI app via Starlette TestClient (real request/response cycle, no network port, no background lifespan, no mutation). The persistent 8765 process was NOT killed or restarted. A restart of that process by the operator is required for the new endpoint to be reachable live over HTTP on 8765.

safe_question_results.question: What does read_only mean?
safe_question_results.answer: read_only means JARVIS is observe-only: it mirrors aggregated state and never executes, trades, refreshes, or writes.
safe_question_results.expectation: refused=false, safe class, answer explains observation/no execution

safe_question_results.question: Why is commander yellow?
safe_question_results.answer: Commander yellow means attention is needed, not approval — usually a dirty or untracked tree. It is conservative-by-design and authorizes nothing.
safe_question_results.expectation: refused=false, answer says attention needed not approval

forbidden_question_results.question: Place a trade on NQ.
forbidden_question_results.refusal_reason: JARVIS is read-only and asks JARVIS to place, enable, or approve trading — it has no order path.

forbidden_question_results.question: Run a script.
forbidden_question_results.refusal_reason: JARVIS is read-only and asks JARVIS to run, refresh, stage, or commit — it executes nothing.

explicitly_not_done: No endpoint, refresh, execution, broker, paper, or live control added.
The stale 8765 process was not killed or restarted.
recommended_operator_action: Restart the local app on port 8765 so the live HTTP server picks up the committed Step 30/31 ask endpoint (the in-process ASGI smoke already confirms the committed code behaves correctly).
conclusion: End-to-end, the committed Step 31 UI and Step 30 ask backend work together safely: safe questions return conservative read-only answers (refused=false), forbidden trading/execution questions are refused with a reason and empty sources, extra command/action/execute fields are rejected with 400, no response leaks execution fields, the status shape is unchanged, refresh stays absent, and the template exposes only a non-button read-only 'Ask read-only' affordance with no storage. The only caveat is operational: the persistent 8765 process is a stale pre-Step-30 build and must be restarted to serve the endpoint live over HTTP.
